questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: 2.505 g of hydrated dibasic acid requires 35 ml of 1 N NaOH solution for complete neutralization. When 1.01 g of the hydrated acid is heated to constant weight 0.72 g of the anhydrous acid is obtained. The degree of hydration (number of water molecules) of the hydrated dibasic acid is approximately _________
questions.solution:
Let formula of hydrated dibasic acid be $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~A} \cdot \mathrm{xH}_2 \mathrm{O}$ Equivalent of dibasic acid = Equivalent of NaOH

$\begin{aligned} & \frac{2.505 \times 2}{M}=\frac{35}{1000} \times 1 \\ & M=\frac{2.505 \times 2 \times 1000}{35 \times 1}=143.14 \\ & \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~A} \cdot \mathrm{xH}_2 \mathrm{O} \xrightarrow{\Delta} \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~A}+\mathrm{xH}_2 \mathrm{O}\end{aligned}$

Mole of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ formed $=x \times$ mole of hydrated dibasic acid

$\begin{aligned} & \frac{(1.01-0.72)}{18}=x \times \frac{1.01}{143.14} \\ & x=2.28\end{aligned}$

degree of hydration is $\approx 2$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A solution of 0.2 g of a compound containing $\mathrm{Cu}^{+2}$ and $\mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{O}_4^{-2}$ ions on titration with $0.02 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{KMnO}_4$ in presence of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ consumes 22.6 mL of the oxidant. The resultant solution is neutralized with $\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3$, acidified with dilute acetic acid and treated with excess KI. The liberated iodine requires 11.3 mL of $0.05 \mathrm{~N} \mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{~S}_2 \mathrm{O}_3$ solution for complete reduction. Find out the molar ratio of $\mathrm{Cu}^{+2}$ to $\mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{O}_4^{-2}$ in the compound. Write down the balanced redox reactions involved in the above titration.
questions.solution:
The mixture of $\mathrm{Cu}^{2+}$ and $\mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{O}_4^{2-}$ are reacting separately first with $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$ solution and then with solid KI to liberate iodine. It can be seen that $\mathrm{Cu}^{+2}$ cannot be oxidised and $\mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{O}_4^{-2}$ cannot be reduced. This is because Cu is already in its highest oxidation state +2 .
$\therefore$ Equivalents of $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$ solution $=\frac{0.02 \times 5 \times 22.6}{1000}=2.26 \times 10^{-3}$
$\therefore$ moles of $\mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{O}_4^{2-}=\frac{2.26 \times 10^{-3}}{2}=1.13 \times 10^{-3}$
This is because only $\mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{O}_4^{-2}$ is oxidised by $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$ to $\mathrm{CO}_2$ (' n ' factor 2)
Equivalents of $\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{~S}_2 \mathrm{O}_3$ solution $=\frac{0.05 \times 11.3}{1000}=5.65 \times 10^{-4}$
$\therefore$ moles of $\mathrm{Cu}^{+2}=\frac{5.65 \times 10^{-4}}{1}=5.65 \times 10^{-4}$
This is because only $\mathrm{Cu}^{+2}$ is reduced by KI to $\mathrm{Cu}^{+}$
(Note: Whenever a metal ion is reduced it always goes to lower oxidation state but generally never goes to oxidation state zero).
$\therefore$ molar ratio of $\mathrm{Cu}^{+2}$ to $\mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{O}_4^{2-}=\frac{5.65 \times 10^{-4}}{1.13 \times 10^{-3}}=0.5$
Reactions:
$\begin{aligned}
& 2 \mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}+5 \mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{O}_4^{-2}+16 \mathrm{H}^{+} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{Mn}^{+2} 10 \mathrm{CO}_2+8 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \\
& \left(2 \mathrm{Cu}^{+2}+4 \mathrm{I}^{-} \rightarrow \mathrm{Cu}_2 \mathrm{I}_2+\mathrm{I}_2\right) \mathrm{I}_2+2 \mathrm{~S}_2 \mathrm{O}_3^{-2} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}^{-}+\mathrm{S}_4 \mathrm{O}_6^{2-} .
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: An equal volume of a reducing agent is treated separately with $1 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{KMnO}_4$ in acid, neutral and alkaline medium. The volumes of $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$ required are 20 ml in acid, 33.4 ml in neutral and 100 ml in alkaline medium. Find out the oxidation state of manganese in each reaction product. Find out the volume of $1 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7$ consumed, if the same volume of the reducing agent is treated in acid medium. (mark the answer in ml)
questions.solution:
Let $\mathrm{N}_1, \mathrm{~N}_2$ and $\mathrm{N}_3$ be the normalitics of 1 M KMnO 4 solution in acidic, neutral and alkaline medium respectively.
$\begin{aligned} 
& 20 \mathrm{ml} \mathrm{~N}_1 \equiv 33.4 \mathrm{ml} \mathrm{~N}_2 \equiv 100 \mathrm{ml} \mathrm{~N}_3
\end{aligned}$
In acidic medium
$1 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{KMnO}_4=5 \mathrm{~N} \mathrm{KMnO}_4$
Thus $\mathrm{N}_2=\frac{20}{33.4} \mathrm{~N}_1=\frac{20}{33.4} \times 5 \mathrm{~N}=3 \mathrm{~N}$
$N_3=\frac{20}{100} N_1=\frac{20 \times 5 \mathrm{~N}}{100}=1 \mathrm{~N}$
The volume required for titration of the same volume of reducing agent with acidified $\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}$, solution
$\begin{aligned} & 20 \mathrm{ml} 5 \mathrm{~N} \mathrm{KMnO}_4=\mathrm{V}_6 \mathrm{NK}_2 \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7 \\ & \mathrm{~V}=\frac{20 \times 5}{6}=16.66 \mathrm{ml}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$ is reduced rapidly by $\mathrm{Sn}^{2+}$, the products being $\mathrm{Sn}^{4+}$ and water. $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$ decomposes slowly at room temperature to yield $\mathrm{O}_2$ and water. Calculate the volume of $\mathrm{O}_2$ produced at $20^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and 1 atm . when 200 gm . of $10 \%$ by mass $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$ in water is treated with 100 ml . of $2 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{Sn}^{2+}$ and then the mixture is allowed to stand until no further reaction occurs. (mark the answer in L)
questions.solution:
Equivalents of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$ intially $=\frac{200 \times 10}{100} \times \frac{1}{34} \times 2=1.176$
Equivalents of $\mathrm{Sn}^{2+} \quad=\frac{2 \times 2 \times 100}{1000}=0.4$
Equivalents of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$ left $\quad=1.176-0.4=0.776$
Moles of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$ left $\quad=\frac{0.776}{2}=0.388$
Moles of $\mathrm{O}_2$ produced $\quad=\frac{0.388}{2}\left[\because \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2 \rightarrow \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_2\right]=0.194$
Volume of $\mathrm{O}_2 \quad=\frac{0.194 \times 0.082 \times 293}{1}=4.66 \mathrm{~L}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: 2 moles of a mixture of $\mathrm{O}_2$ and $\mathrm{O}_3$ is reacted with excess of acidified solution of KI . The iodine liberated require 1 L of 2 M hypo solution for complete reaction. The weight $\%$ of $\mathrm{O}_3$ in the initial sample is x . Find x .
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{KI}+\mathrm{O}_3 \longrightarrow \mathrm{I}_2+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}+\mathrm{O}_2$
$v . f=2 \quad v . f=2$
$\begin{aligned} & \text { moles } \\ & =\mathrm{x} \text {. }\end{aligned}$
$\mathrm{I}_2+\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{~S}_2 \mathrm{O}_3 \longrightarrow \mathrm{I}^{-}+\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{~S}_4 \mathrm{O}_6$
$\begin{array}{ll}\mathrm{v} . \mathrm{f}=2 & M=2 \\ & V=1 \mathrm{~L} \\ \mathrm{v} . f=1\end{array}$
$\begin{aligned} & \text { eq of } \mathrm{O}_3=\text { eq of } \mathrm{I}_2=\mathrm{eq} \text { of hypo } \\ & \Rightarrow \quad 2 \times x=2 \\ & x=1 \text { mole }\end{aligned}$
$\Rightarrow \quad$ weight $\%$ of $O_3=\frac{1 \times 48}{1 \times 48+1 \times 32} \times 100$
$=\frac{3}{5} \times 100=60 \%$ Ans. 60
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
The equivalent weight of $\mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{OH})_2$ in the following reaction is equal to its $\mathrm{M} / \mathrm{x}$ :
$\mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{OH})_2+\mathrm{HNO}_3 \rightarrow \mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{OH})\left(\mathrm{NO}_3\right)+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$
X is equal to
questions.solution:
E.W. $=\frac{\text { M.W }}{\text { acidity }}$
$\mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{OH})_2$ can replace only one OH .
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: 1 g of an acid $\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_{10} \mathrm{O}_4$ requires 0.768 g of KOH for complete neutralization. Determine the basicity of acid.
questions.solution:
Let basicity of acid be $n$.
Equivalent of acid = Equivalent of KOH

$\begin{aligned} & \frac{1 \times n}{146}=\frac{0.768}{56} \\ & n=\frac{0.768 \times 146}{56}=2\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: 22.21 ml solution of $\mathrm{HIO}_3$ is reacted with excess of anaqueous solution of $\mathrm{SO}_2$. The excess of $\mathrm{SO}_2$ and $\mathrm{I}_2$ formed are removed by heating the solution. The remaining solution in neutralized by 35.5 ml of $0.16(\mathrm{~N}) \mathrm{NaOHsolution}$. The strength of $\mathrm{HIO}_3 \mathrm{in} \mathrm{gm} / \mathrm{lt}$. (Round off the answer to the nearest whole number) is...?
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& 22.21 \times N_{\mathrm{HIO}_3}=35.5 \times 0.16 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad N_{\mathrm{HIO}_3}=\frac{35.5 \times 0.16}{22.21}=0.256(\mathrm{~N})
\end{aligned}$

N factor of $\mathrm{HIO}_3=5$
Mass of $\mathrm{HIO}_3=0.256 \times \frac{176}{5} \approx 9 \mathrm{gm} / \mathrm{lt}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
